anchor.value: {\displaystyle \forall X\left[\varnothing \in X\lor \exists f\left[\mathrm {dom} \ f=X\land \forall A\in X\left[f\left(A\right)\in A\right]\right]\right].}

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the ZFC choice function via ZFSet.choice with choice_isFunc/choice_mem, not the raw first-order sentence.

anchor.value: {\displaystyle {\begin{aligned}\forall x(&\\&\exists e(e\in x\land \lnot \exists y(y\in e))\lor \\&\exists a\,\exists b\,\exists c\,(a\in x\land b\in x\land c\in a\land c\in b\land \lnot (a=b))\lor \\&\exists c\,\forall e\,(e\in x\implies \exists a\,(a\in e\land a\in c\land \forall b\,((b\in e\land b\in c)\implies a=b))))\end{aligned}}}

annotations.note: Zermelo's pairwise-disjoint-sets variant with a transversal singleton is not formalized as a standalone Mathlib statement.
This is exactly the type-theoretic AC captured by Classical.axiomOfChoice (∀x, ∃y, R x y) → ∃ f, ∀ x, R x (f x).

anchor.value: Axiom — For any set X {\displaystyle X} of nonempty sets, th

annotations.note: The English statement of AC corresponds directly to Classical.axiomOfChoice in Mathlib (and alias axiom_of_choice).
